items.title: "OncoAgent: A Dual-Tier Multi-Agent Framework for Privacy-Preserving Oncology Clinical Decision Support"
The new Wild West of AI kids’ toys
items.summary: These connected companions could disrupt everything from make-believe to bedtime stories. No wonder some lawmakers want them banned.

items.title: [AINews] Anthropic growing 10x/year while everyone else is laying off >10% of their workforce
items.summary: A quiet day lets us reflect on an interesting dichotomy in the economy.

items.title: Quoting Luke Curley
items.summary:
WebRTC is designed to degrade and drop my prompt during poor network conditions.
wtf my dude
WebRTC aggressively drops audio packets to keep latency low. If you’ve ever heard distorted audio on a conference call, that’s WebRTC baybee. The idea is that conference calls depend on rapid back-and-forth, so pausing to wait for audio is unacceptable.
…but as a user, I would much rather wait an extra 200ms for my slow/expensive prompt to be accurate. After all, I’m paying good money to boil the ocean,

items.title: Musk v. Altman week 2: OpenAI fires back, and Shivon Zilis reveals that Musk tried to poach Sam Altman
items.summary: In the second week of the landmark trial between Elon Musk and OpenAI, Musk’s motivations for bringing the suit were under scrutiny. Last week, Musk took the stand, alleging that OpenAI CEO Sam Altman and president Greg Brockman had deceived him into donating $38 million to the company. He claimed that they’d promised to maintain…

items.title: Using Claude Code: The Unreasonable Effectiveness of HTML
items.summary:
Using Claude Code: The Unreasonable Effectiveness of HTML
Thought-provoking piece by Thariq Shihipar (on the Claude Code team at Anthropic) advocating for HTML over Markdown as an output format to request from Claude.
The article is crammed with interesting examples (collected on this site) and prompt suggestions like this one:
